description: tn36 5/7 solved. Player-piano programming game: 2-bit encoding per slot, binary column values index command map. Right panel is editable (not left). L6-L7 unsolved — path puzzle needs mechanic not yet understood.

TWO PANELS: left is read-only context, right is editable program. Binary encoding: N arrow toggles per column, value = sum(2^i). Maps to command table: 0=NOP, 1=LEFT, 2=RIGHT, 3=DOWN, 33=UP, 5=ROT90, 8=SCALE+, 9=SCALE-, 10=dRIGHT, 63=COLOR. Click confirm to play right panel program. Block resets after each play — win checked at end.

ANIMATION DISCOVERY: SDK returns multi-frame tensor (N,64,64) for animation steps. All frames in one response. GIF capture + viewer display working. Animation plays once in viewer then swaps to static frame.

L6 solved via 4-play waypoint chaining. Checkered squares are WAYPOINTS — block docks and saves position when landing on one. Next play starts from saved position. Path: (33,28)→(53,28)→(49,20)→(53,4)→(37,8).

WAYPOINT MECHANIC: checkered 4x4 sprites (ccjcukzyce) are docking points. When block lands exactly on one (same scale), the block home position UPDATES. Block still resets after play, but resets to NEW home. This enables multi-play paths around walls that cant be traversed in 6 steps. Key visual: checkered pattern squares on the grid = plan your route through them.

tn36 COMPLETE 7/7. Player-piano programming game. Key discoveries: binary bit encoding for commands, right panel is editable (not left), checkered squares are waypoints for multi-play paths, portals toggle on step%3==2 with inclusive-bound collision — must time passage through kill zone.
L7: 4-play waypoint path dodging portal kill zone at y=16. Route: (41,28)→(53,24)→(53,8)→(33,12)→(41,8). Portal collision uses inclusive bounds — adjacent sprites count as colliding.
